clickToDetail: Click any item to view details.
shortcutNote: Some shortcuts only work when the image grid has focus (click on the grid or an image first).

albums.title: Albums
albums.description: Create and manage albums, organize and categorize images

wallpaper.title: Wallpaper
wallpaper.description: Set desktop wallpaper, rotation and display mode

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Plugin installation, store sources and plugin editor

tray.title: Tray
tray.description: System tray icon location and functions

virtual-driver.title: Virtual Drive (VD)
virtual-driver.description: Browse albums like a disk in Explorer (Windows)

command-line.title: Command Line
command-line.description: Run plugins, pack and import plugins via CLI

easter-egg.title: Easter Egg
easter-egg.description: Small surprises that don't affect functionality

import-drag-drop.title: Quick import by dragging local files
import-drag-drop.summary: Drag into window or Gallery → Start → Local to import images/folders. Desktop supports drag & file picker; Android uses media picker.

gallery-browsing.title: Gallery browsing methods
gallery-browsing.summary: Learn how to view total count, paginate through large collections, and use deduplication.

gallery-preview.title: In-app double-click preview
gallery-preview.summary: When enabled, double-click any image to view full size in-app with zoom, drag, and navigation.

albums-introduction.title: Albums overview
albums-introduction.summary: Albums organize and categorize images. Create albums, add images, set as wallpaper rotation source.

wallpaper-basic.title: Basic wallpaper setup
wallpaper-basic.summary: Right-click image → "Set as wallpaper" for quick single wallpaper.

wallpaper-rotation.title: Rotation vs non-rotation
wallpaper-rotation.summary: Learn the difference between rotation and single wallpaper modes, and how to enable/disable.

wallpaper-mode.title: Native vs window mode
wallpaper-mode.summary: Native mode: better performance. Window mode: more features. Windows/macOS support window mode.

wallpaper-window-video.title: Window mode, video wallpaper, and app exit
wallpaper-window-video.summary: Window mode supports video/GIF wallpaper. Wallpaper disappears when app exits; keep running to persist.

plugin-introduction.title: What are plugins?
plugin-introduction.summary: Plugins are Kabegame's core feature for collecting images from websites. Learn about formats, types, and usage.

plugin-store-basics.title: Plugin store and store sources
plugin-store-basics.summary: Install/update plugins in Source management; add store sources for installable lists.

plugin-import.title: Plugin import methods
plugin-import.summary: Four ways to import .kgpg: double-click, drag into window, import from Source page, or via plugin editor.

plugin-usage.title: How to collect images with plugins
plugin-usage.summary: Open collect dialog from Gallery, select plugin, configure params, set output dir and album, then start.

task-introduction.title: Task overview
task-introduction.summary: Learn what tasks are, what they do, max concurrent tasks, and download limits.

task-viewing.title: View task info, status and images
task-viewing.summary: Learn how to view task info, status and collected images via task drawer and detail page.

task-management.title: Create, delete and stop tasks
task-management.summary: Learn how to create tasks, stop running tasks, and delete tasks (including batch).

tray-introduction.title: Tray location and functions
tray-introduction.summary: Learn tray icon location, left/right click behavior, and menu options.

virtual-driver-basics.title: Browse images via virtual drive in Explorer
virtual-driver-basics.summary: Enable "Album drive" to browse albums and images as files in Explorer.

virtual-driver-directories.title: Virtual drive directory structure
virtual-driver-directories.summary: Learn the purpose of each directory (All, By plugin, By time, By task, Albums).

command-line-basics.title: CLI basics
command-line-basics.summary: Learn Kabegame CLI: run plugins, pack plugins, import plugins.

command-line-examples.title: Common command examples
command-line-examples.summary: Practical examples: import single image, import folder to gallery, etc.

easter-egg-turtle-complaint.title: Fast scrolling triggers turtle complaint
easter-egg-turtle-complaint.summary: Scroll too fast in gallery/album and a playful "turtle can't keep up" message appears.

shortcutGridZoomWheelDesc: Hold Ctrl (Cmd on macOS) and scroll to adjust image grid columns
shortcutGridZoomPlusMinusDesc: Hold Ctrl (Cmd on macOS) and press +/- or = to adjust columns
shortcutSelectAllDesc: Select all images on the current page in the grid
shortcutClearSelectionDesc: Clear selection and close context menu
shortcutDeleteDesc: Backspace hides selected images; Delete deletes them (triggers app delete flow/confirm)
shortcutRangeSelectDesc: Hold Shift and click to select range from last selection
shortcutToggleSelectDesc: Hold Ctrl (Cmd on macOS) and click to toggle image selection
shortcutCopyImageDesc: Copy current image to clipboard (preview or grid)
shortcutPreviewDeleteDesc: Backspace hides the current preview image; Delete deletes it (triggers delete flow)
noHelpContent: No help content on this page

import-drag-drop.overviewPara: Local import is built in—you do not need a separate "local import" plugin. Add local images to the gallery as follows:
import-drag-drop.overviewItem1: Desktop: drag images, folders, or archives into the window, or open Gallery → Start collecting → Local from the top-right.
import-drag-drop.overviewItem2: Android: tap Start collecting → Local in the top-right of the gallery, then pick images in the system media picker.
import-drag-drop.desktopDragPara1: Drag image files, folders containing images, or image archives (zip) into the main window.
import-drag-drop.desktopDragPara2: You can mix types in one drop: multiple files, folders, archives, or any combination. The app shows everything in one import confirmation dialog.
import-drag-drop.desktopDragPara3: An import confirmation dialog appears; you can create an album per source (recommended for large batches).
import-drag-drop.desktopStartCollectPara: On the gallery page, click Start collecting (top-right), choose Local, then use Add files / Add folders. You can still create an album per source.
import-drag-drop.androidStartCollectPara: Tap Start collecting (top-right), choose Local, pick images in the media picker, confirm—the app creates an import task and adds images to the gallery.
import-drag-drop.folderPara1: Dropping a folder recursively scans subfolders and imports supported images.
import-drag-drop.folderPara2: If "Create an album per source" is enabled, a same-named album is created and results go there.
import-drag-drop.zipPara1: Dropping a zip imports images inside without manual extraction.
import-drag-drop.zipPara2: With "Create an album per source", a same-named album is created for that zip.
import-drag-drop.macosTitle: macOS: importing Pictures, Desktop, etc. fails
import-drag-drop.macosPara1: On macOS, Pictures, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, etc. may be protected. Drag-and-drop may be denied ("permission denied").
import-drag-drop.macosItem1: Prefer Add folder and select the folder in the system picker, then import.
import-drag-drop.macosItem2: If it still fails: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Files and Folders—grant Kabegame access (e.g. Pictures).
import-drag-drop.builtinTitle: Note: remove the old local-import plugin; the feature is built in
import-drag-drop.builtinPara: Local import is built into the app and no longer depends on a separate local-import source plugin. If you installed it separately, remove it in Source management to avoid confusion.
import-drag-drop.builtinNote: Removing the old plugin is safe; drag-in and Start collecting → Local keep working.

gallery-browsing.totalPara1: The gallery toolbar subtitle shows how many images you are browsing.
gallery-browsing.totalItem2: Pagination on: shows "X / Y" (X = current position, Y = total images).
gallery-browsing.totalPara2: The count updates when you delete, import, or deduplicate.
gallery-browsing.paginationPara1: When the gallery has more than 100 images, pagination turns on automatically.
gallery-browsing.paginationPara2: The paginator appears below the gallery toolbar and includes:
gallery-browsing.paginationItem1: Prev / next: jump between adjacent pages
gallery-browsing.paginationItem2: Page input: jump to a page by number
gallery-browsing.paginationItem3: Page count: current page and total pages
gallery-browsing.organizePara1: The gallery toolbar has an Organize button (filter icon) for maintenance.
gallery-browsing.organizeItem1: Deduplicate: remove duplicates by file hash (SHA256); same content is removed even if filenames differ
gallery-browsing.organizeItem2: Remove missing: drop records whose files no longer exist on disk
gallery-browsing.organizeItem3: Regenerate thumbnails: generate thumbnails where missing
gallery-browsing.organizePara3: After Organize, pick actions in the dialog. Work runs in the background and can be cancelled.
gallery-browsing.organizeNote: Organize applies immediately—choose options that match your needs.

gallery-preview.featurePara1: In-app double-click preview lets you open large images inside Kabegame without the system viewer—in the gallery and albums.
gallery-preview.featurePara2: When enabled, double-click any image to open the preview with:
gallery-preview.featureItem1: Large view: original size or fit to window
gallery-preview.featureItem2: Zoom: mouse wheel or trackpad gestures
gallery-preview.featureItem4: Navigate: arrow keys or on-screen buttons for prev/next
gallery-preview.featureItem5: Quick actions: delete, add to album, etc.
gallery-preview.enableStep2: Go to the App settings tab
gallery-preview.enableNote: If you choose Open with system default, double-click uses the external viewer instead.
gallery-preview.usagePara1: Double-click an image in the gallery or an album to open preview.
gallery-preview.usagePara2: In the preview dialog you can:
gallery-preview.usageClose: Close: click outside the dialog or press Esc
gallery-preview.usageSwitchItem3: Hover near the left/right edge for semi-transparent buttons
gallery-preview.usageDelete: Hide / delete: Backspace hides the current image; Delete removes it
gallery-preview.scenariosPara: In-app preview is great when you want to:
gallery-preview.scenariosItem1: Browse quickly without opening/closing an external viewer
gallery-preview.scenariosItem2: Review many images with keyboard navigation
gallery-preview.scenariosItem3: Inspect details with smooth zoom and pan
gallery-preview.scenariosItem4: Act on images (delete, add to album) without returning to the grid

albums-introduction.whatPara1: Albums organize and group images—like playlists or folders. Create multiple albums for themes, sources, or uses.
albums-introduction.whatItem1: Gallery: all collected images, browsed by plugin, time, etc.
albums-introduction.whatItem2: Albums: you curate which images belong in each album
albums-introduction.whatPara3: One image can be in many albums; albums reference files—they do not duplicate them.
albums-introduction.createPara1: On the Albums page (sidebar), click New album (top-right), enter a name.
albums-introduction.createPara2: Names are up to 50 characters—pick something easy to find later.
albums-introduction.addItem1: From gallery: select images, right-click Add to album, pick or create an album
albums-introduction.addItem2: From album detail: select images, right-click Add to album to move to another album
albums-introduction.addItem3: From task detail: select images, right-click Add to album
albums-introduction.addItem4: On import: choose Create an album per source when dragging files/folders
albums-introduction.addPara2: Duplicates in the same album are skipped.
albums-introduction.managePara1: On the album list you can:
albums-introduction.manageItem1: Open: click a card to see all images
albums-introduction.manageItem2: Rename: double-click the title on the detail page (or edit mode)
albums-introduction.manageItem3: Delete album: Delete album on the detail page (removes the album record, not image files)
albums-introduction.manageItem4: Slideshow wallpaper: Set as slideshow source from album detail
albums-introduction.removePara1: On album detail, right-click Remove from album:
albums-introduction.removeItem1: Remove from album only: keeps files and other albums (recommended)
albums-introduction.removeItem2: Also delete files (desktop only): permanently deletes files—cannot be undone
albums-introduction.removeNote: Also delete files is irreversible—try remove-only first.
albums-introduction.wallpaperItem1: On album detail, Set as slideshow wallpaper uses that album as the source
albums-introduction.wallpaperItem2: Under Settings → Wallpaper slideshow you can pick an album too
albums-introduction.wallpaperItem3: Slideshow picks images in order or randomly from the album
albums-introduction.wallpaperPara2: If you delete an album used by slideshow, slideshow stops and returns to single wallpaper.

wallpaper-basic.contextTitle: Set wallpaper from the image menu
wallpaper-basic.contextPara1: In gallery, albums, or task detail, right-click an image and choose Set as wallpaper to apply it to the desktop.
wallpaper-basic.contextPara2: Fastest way to set a single wallpaper without opening Settings.
wallpaper-basic.otherItem1: Settings: Settings → Wallpaper slideshow → Choose wallpaper (when slideshow is off)
wallpaper-basic.otherItem2: Album detail: Set as slideshow wallpaper (enables slideshow)
wallpaper-basic.noteItem2: If slideshow is on, setting a single image turns slideshow off

wallpaper-rotation.modePara: Kabegame supports slideshow mode and single-image mode.
wallpaper-rotation.nonRotationItem2: Change manually (e.g. right-click → Set as wallpaper)
wallpaper-rotation.nonRotationItem3: Best when you want one image to stay on the desktop
wallpaper-rotation.rotationItem1: Rotates through images from a chosen album
wallpaper-rotation.rotationItem4: Best when you want the desktop to change automatically
wallpaper-rotation.toggleItem1: On: Settings → Wallpaper slideshow → enable, then pick an album
wallpaper-rotation.toggleItem2: Off: disable in Settings, or set a single wallpaper from an image (slideshow stops)
wallpaper-rotation.toggleItem3: From album detail: Set as slideshow wallpaper enables slideshow with that album
wallpaper-rotation.optionsItem2b: Random: pick a random image each time
wallpaper-rotation.optionsItem3: Transitions: animations when changing (slideshow only; same set for images and video)
wallpaper-rotation.noteItem1: Slideshow needs a non-empty album as the source
wallpaper-rotation.noteItem2: Deleting an album in use stops slideshow and returns to single wallpaper
wallpaper-rotation.noteItem3: Setting a single wallpaper from an image turns slideshow off

wallpaper-mode.comparePara: Kabegame can render wallpaper in native mode or window mode—trade-offs differ.
wallpaper-mode.nativeItem1: Better performance: system APIs, lower overhead
wallpaper-mode.nativeItem2: Fewer transitions: usually none or fade only
wallpaper-mode.nativeItem4: Fit modes depend on the OS (fill, fit, stretch, …)
wallpaper-mode.windowItem1: Richer effects: similar to Wallpaper Engine—all transitions
wallpaper-mode.windowItem2: Slightly heavier: transparent window over the desktop
wallpaper-mode.windowItem3: Windows / macOS: desktop supports window mode; Android uses system wallpaper APIs
wallpaper-mode.windowItem4: All fit modes: fill, fit, stretch, center, tile, …
wallpaper-mode.switchPara2: Switching reapplies the current wallpaper immediately.
wallpaper-mode.transitionNative2: Slide, zoom, etc. are unavailable in native mode
wallpaper-mode.transitionWindow1: All transitions: none, fade, slide, zoom, … (images and video)
wallpaper-mode.transitionWindow2: Transitions apply when slideshow changes images
wallpaper-mode.suggestItem3: Single static image: either works; native is lighter

wallpaper-window-video.videoTitle: Video and animated wallpaper in window mode
wallpaper-window-video.videoPara1: In window mode you can use videos or GIFs as wallpaper—rendered full-screen behind the desktop.
wallpaper-window-video.videoItem1: Windows: MP4 as wallpaper in window mode; GIF may use native mode.
wallpaper-window-video.videoItem2: macOS: video and GIF play in the desktop layer window.
wallpaper-window-video.videoPara2: If you are in native mode and pick media that needs window mode, the app asks you to switch first—or change mode under Settings → Wallpaper slideshow, then set the wallpaper.
wallpaper-window-video.closeTitle: Wallpaper disappears when the app quits
wallpaper-window-video.closePara1: Wallpaper is provided by Kabegame. After a full quit (tray exit, close and exit, etc.) the desktop may revert to the previous wallpaper—this is expected.
wallpaper-window-video.closeItem1: Window mode draws via an app window; native mode may also revert on quit on some systems.
wallpaper-window-video.closeItem2: To keep it: leave Kabegame running (e.g. minimize to tray, don’t choose Quit).

plugin-introduction.whatPara1: Plugins (also called "sources") are how Kabegame collects images from websites.
plugin-introduction.whatPara2: Each plugin defines how to crawl a site: URLs, image links, metadata, etc.
plugin-introduction.whatPara3: Install different plugins to collect from many places into one gallery and albums.
plugin-introduction.formatPara2: Edit or author plugins in the plugin editor.
plugin-introduction.typeItem1: Web crawlers: online sites (e.g. Konachan, Anihonet)
plugin-introduction.typeItem2: Local import: import from disk (e.g. built-in local-import)
plugin-introduction.typeItem3: Custom: your own plugins for supported targets
plugin-introduction.roleItem1: Collect from many sites in one place
plugin-introduction.roleItem3: Save to a folder and add to gallery/albums
plugin-introduction.rolePara2: Without plugins you cannot collect—installing plugins is the first step.
plugin-introduction.getItem1: Install from a store: Source management → browse store sources
plugin-introduction.getItem2: Import manually: drag .kgpg, double-click, or import from Source management
plugin-introduction.getItem3: Write your own: plugin editor + Rhai
plugin-introduction.getNote: local-import is preinstalled for local import. If you remove it, drag-to-import stops working.

plugin-store-basics.entryPara: Open Source management from the sidebar to manage source plugins (crawl/import).
plugin-store-basics.tabsItem1: Installed: plugins on this device—view or uninstall.
plugin-store-basics.tabsItem2: Store: tabs per source name with remote lists—install or update.
plugin-store-basics.addPara1: Use the Add source tab at the bottom to add a store URL.
plugin-store-basics.addPara2: A store is an index.json URL (often a GitHub Releases asset link).
plugin-store-basics.addNote: A new tab appears; opening it loads that source’s plugin list lazily.
plugin-store-basics.installPara1: Pick a plugin in a store tab—after install it appears under Installed.
plugin-store-basics.installPara2: If you previously imported a .kgpg, the store shows the installed version for updates.
plugin-store-basics.editorPara: Top-right Plugin editor for developing and debugging plugins.

plugin-import.method1Para1: Double-click a .kgpg in File Explorer to open import.
plugin-import.method1Para2: Fastest from Downloads or cloud folders.
plugin-import.method1Note: Windows may ask "Open with" once—choose Kabegame; file association can follow.
plugin-import.method2Para1: Drop .kgpg into the main window—the app imports it.
plugin-import.method2Para2: You can drop multiple files at once.
plugin-import.method3Para2: Pick a .kgpg in the file dialog.
plugin-import.method4Item6: Import from the editor or use the methods above
plugin-import.method4Note: Full dev workflow for custom sources or forks.
plugin-import.afterItem2: If the same name exists, you may overwrite/update
plugin-import.afterItem3: Manage under Installed in Source management

plugin-usage.openPara1: Gallery → Collect (top-right), or Start importing when the gallery is empty.
plugin-usage.openPara2: Main place to configure a plugin and start a collection task.
plugin-usage.selectPara1: Pick an installed plugin from the source dropdown.
plugin-usage.selectNote: If the list is empty, install plugins from a store first.
plugin-usage.configPara1: If the plugin defines variables, they appear in the form.
plugin-usage.outputPara1: Output folder: where files save; empty uses the default download folder (Settings).
plugin-usage.outputPara2: Output album: add to an album or + New album; empty adds to gallery only.
plugin-usage.outputPara3: For local-import you can create an album per folder/archive.
plugin-usage.savePara1: Save the current configuration for reuse.
plugin-usage.savePara2: Next time, pick the preset instead of retyping.
plugin-usage.savePara3: Stores: plugin, variables, output folder (if set).
plugin-usage.saveNote: If a plugin updates and breaks compatibility, a preset may show Incompatible—reconfigure.
plugin-usage.startPara1: Click Start collecting to create the task.
plugin-usage.startItem1: Task drawer (bottom-right) shows list and progress
plugin-usage.startItem2: States: pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled
plugin-usage.startItem3: Open a task for images and failure logs
plugin-usage.startPara3: When done, files are saved and added to gallery (and album if set).
plugin-usage.faqA1: A: You may hit max concurrent downloads (Settings). New tasks queue until slots free.
plugin-usage.faqA2: A: Open the task for errors—network, bad config, bad URL, etc. Fix and retry failed items.

task-introduction.whatPara1: A task is one collection run. It includes:
task-introduction.whatItem1: Runs a source plugin against a site or local paths
task-introduction.whatItem2: Uses the parameters you set (keywords, page, URL, …)
task-introduction.whatItem4: Adds to gallery and album (if chosen)
task-introduction.whatPara2: Tasks run in the background—watch them in the task drawer.
task-introduction.doStep1Para: The Rhai script fetches lists from the site or scans local paths.
task-introduction.doStep2Para: Network plugins download files; local plugins scan and import.
task-introduction.doStep3Para: Files go under your output folder (or default), grouped by plugin id.
task-introduction.doStep4Para: Completed downloads appear in the gallery and optional album.
task-introduction.doStep5Para: Counts, progress, failures are recorded in the task.
task-introduction.maxPara1: Up to 10 tasks may run; extra tasks stay pending.
task-introduction.maxItem1: Max concurrent downloads (Settings → Download, 1–10) across all tasks
task-introduction.maxItem2: That limit is total parallel downloads, not per task
task-introduction.maxItem3: Example: limit 5 means at most 5 images downloading even with many running tasks
task-introduction.maxItem4: New tasks wait if the download cap is hit
task-introduction.maxItem5: When a slot frees, pending tasks start
task-introduction.maxItem6: States you may see: pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled
task-introduction.maxNote: If tasks stay pending, you can:
task-introduction.maxNoteItem2: Raise max concurrent downloads (watch network stability)
task-introduction.typePara2: Flow is similar; only the data source differs.

task-viewing.drawerPara1: Gallery / albums: task button (top-right) opens the drawer with all tasks.
task-viewing.drawerItem2: State: pending, running, completed, failed, cancelled
task-viewing.detailPara1: Click a task or right-click View details.
task-viewing.statusPara2: Detail view shows the current state and logs.
task-viewing.imagesPara: On the detail page you can:
task-viewing.imagesItem5: Delete from the task view without deleting downloaded files (unless you choose otherwise)

task-management.createMethod2: B) Drag files into the app
task-management.createMethod2Para: Dragging images, folders, or zip creates a local import task:
task-management.createMethod2Item4: Optional album per source in the confirm dialog
task-management.stopItem1: Already downloaded files remain; not-yet-started work is cancelled
task-management.deleteItem1: Running tasks are stopped first, then removed
task-management.deleteItem2: Only the task record is removed—files on disk stay
task-management.deleteItem4: Completed tasks can be deleted without losing files
task-management.batchItem1: Clear finished: remove completed/failed/cancelled in one action
task-management.batchNote: Batch delete still does not delete downloaded files.

tray-introduction.positionPara1: Kabegame shows an icon in the system tray (near the clock).
tray-introduction.leftNote: Left click does not open the menu—use right click for the menu.
tray-introduction.sceneItem3: Change wallpaper without opening the main window
tray-introduction.sceneItem4: Fully exit (closing the window often only hides)
tray-introduction.notePara1: Closing the main window usually hides the app instead of quitting.
tray-introduction.notePara2: Use Quit from the tray menu to exit completely.

virtual-driver-basics.whatPara1: Virtual Drive (VD) mounts your library as a drive letter in File Explorer.
virtual-driver-basics.whatPara2: Browse albums and files like normal folders—drag, copy paths, open in other apps.
virtual-driver-basics.enablePara2: Default mount is often K:\ (see your settings).
virtual-driver-basics.openItem3: Or type the drive letter in the address bar, e.g. K:\
virtual-driver-basics.notePara1: VD is an on-demand view—some advanced file ops may not apply.
virtual-driver-basics.notePara2: If you don’t see the drive, check Album drive is on and the letter isn’t taken.

virtual-driver-directories.rootPara: Open the mount (default K:\) to see:
virtual-driver-directories.allPara1: All gallery images, paged (~100 per page).
virtual-driver-directories.pluginPara2: One folder per plugin with its images.
virtual-driver-directories.taskPara2: Folder name: task name - task id.
virtual-driver-directories.albumNote: Some file ops may be allowed; image files are often read-only.
virtual-driver-directories.readmeTitle: 在这里你可以自由查看图片.txt
virtual-driver-directories.readmePara1: Readme at the root explaining VD.
virtual-driver-directories.notePara1: VD is for browsing; not all file manager features apply.
virtual-driver-directories.notePara2: Advanced ops may be limited—follow in-app behavior.

command-line-basics.whatPara1: Kabegame ships kabegame-cli for terminal-based plugin operations.
command-line-basics.whatPara2: Useful for scripts, batch jobs, or headless runs.
command-line-basics.startPara1: Usually next to the app install—kabegame-cli or kabegame-cli.exe.
command-line-basics.startPara3: Shows help for commands and flags.
command-line-basics.runPara: Run an installed plugin or a .kgpg:
command-line-basics.runParam5: After -- : values mapped to plugin variables
command-line-basics.notePara1: CLI needs a correct install and plugin/config paths.
command-line-basics.notePara2: Pass plugin args after -- as required.

command-line-examples.commonPara: Copy and edit paths for your machine.
command-line-examples.outputPara2: Without --output-dir, default download folder is used.
command-line-examples.albumPara2: Without --output-album-id, images go to gallery only.
command-line-examples.noteItem1: local-import should be installed (bundled by default)
command-line-examples.noteNote: "Plugin not found" → install local-import from the store.

easter-egg-turtle-complaint.whatPara: Scroll very fast in a grid and you may see a playful line such as:
easter-egg-turtle-complaint.triggerItem1: Gallery: fast mouse wheel—may show a random line when speed is high.
easter-egg-turtle-complaint.triggerItem2: Album detail: fast drag-scroll while accelerating may show a shorter line.
easter-egg-turtle-complaint.descPara: Pure easter egg—does not affect loading. Every image deserves a calm look.
